missing_removal_handling.policy: Missing per-criterion train values are kept as NULL/unknown, MEDIAN-IMPUTED ONLY for TOPSIS geometry, flagged with data_status='unknown_median_imputed', and they REDUCE confidence. They are NOT treated as 0% removal.
missing_removal_handling.evidence: See c_decision_matrix.csv data_status column; train 7 (On-site disposal) C1/C2/C7 are unknown_median_imputed, not zero.

onsite_disposal_hard_filter_check.question: Was on-site disposal hard-filtered, and on what (soil/infiltration)?
onsite_disposal_hard_filter_check.answer: NO. Train 7 (On-site disposal = Septic + Soak Pit/Soakaway; component nbs_id 20, family 'Infiltration & Soil Systems') was NOT hard-filtered. It survived to ranking and placed last only because its discharge_inland use-case verdict is 'unknown' (no canonical performance evidence), with low confidence (0.45).
why_not_filtered_even_though_a_rule_exis: A relevant HARD rule exists: APP_RULE_023 (rule_type=hard_filter, family 'Infiltration & Soil Systems', factor soil_infiltration = 'low_infiltration_or_rocky_or_shallow_soil', action remove_infiltration_system).
The site IS low-infiltration: Indirasagar region 27 infiltration_class='low', HSG 'D', Clay soil.

Gap 1 (train path): train_recommendation._site_rule_context() passes only stream_order/slope/builtup_frac/agri_frac/dom_land_cover to the rule context — it does NOT pass soil_infiltration, so APP_RULE_023 can never fire during train ranking.

Gap 2 (option path): applicability _factor_matches compares normalize(infiltration_class)=='low' against the rule token 'low_infiltration_or_rocky_or_shallow_soil'; there is no value->category mapping, so the literal equality fails and the rule does not fire there either.
Net: on-site disposal/soak-pit is a DISCLOSED GAP here, not a soil-validated pass. On low-infiltration clay it should arguably be cautioned/removed.
